title: Analytical application of 3-hydroxy-3-p-tolyl-1-m-tolyltriazene in the spectrophotometric determination of molybdenum(VI)
author: Tushita Babel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, M. L. Sukhadia University, Udaipur-313 001, India 
 E-mail  : akumargoswami@rediffmail.com 
 Manuscript received 14 October 2003. accepted 4 March 2004  

 A new reagent 3-hydroxy-3- p -tolyl-1- m -tolyltriazene has been used for the spectrophotometric determination of Mo VI  at 410 
nm, in the pH range 2.3-3.3. Beer's law is obeyed in the range (1 to 6) x 10 -5   M . The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity values are 8004 dm 3  mol -1  cm -1  and 11.98 ng cm -2 , respectively. The method is useful even in presence of several 
cations and anions.
